hypothesis: Correcting aromatic valency handling and training generative models on Kekulized, chemically accurate datasets will produce generated 3D molecular structures that are closer to GFN2-xTB-optimized geometries—exhibiting significantly lower relaxation energies (ΔE_relax) and smaller geometric deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ)—than models trained on the original datasets with the valency bug, and evaluations using GFN2-xTB will report substantially lower relaxation energies than MMFF94-based evaluations.
